BITD.Title: Title
BITD.Warning: Be aware, this will clear all Heat/Wanted
BITD.SquadTypeBox: Click to choose a Squad Type
BITD.ChooseOneOrTwoEdges: Choose one or two edges and an equal number of flaws
BITD.AddAdditionalTypeOfGangText:
You can add an additional type to a fireteam or expert by spending two squad upgrades.
When a cohort performs actions for which its types apply, it uses its full quality rating. Otherwise, its quality is zero. A given cohort can have up to two types. Some squad upgrades will add the “Elite” feature to a fireteam, which gives them +1d when they roll for a given Type.
BITD.ClassExpClue1: Every time you roll a desperate action, mark xp in that action's attribute.
BITD.ClassExpClue2: You expressed your beliefs, drive, history, or opening.
BITD.ClassExpClue3: You struggled with issues from your tragedy or scars during the session.
BITD.ClassExpClueDescription: At the end of each session, for each item below, mark 1 XP (in your playbook or an attribute) or 2 XP if that item occurred multiple times.
BITD.HoldVendettaEffect: Your Squad's Hold has currently been lowered because of your Vendetta with the Squad(s) {vendettas}.
BITD.TierVendettaEffect: Your Squad's Tier has currently been lowered because of your Vendetta with the Squad(s) {vendettas}.
BITD.SquadExpClue1: At the end of each session, for each item below, mark 1 XP (or mark 2 XP if that item occurred multiple times).
BITD.EdgesFearsomeDescription: The cohort is terrifying in aspect and reputation.
BITD.EdgesIndependentDescription: The cohort can be trusted to make good decisions and act on their own initiative in the absence of direct orders.
BITD.EdgesLoyalDescription: The cohort can't be bribed or turned against you.
BITD.EdgesTenaciousDescription: The cohort won't be deterred from a task.
BITD.FlawsPrincipledDescription: The cohort has an ethic or values that it won't betray.
BITD.FlawsSavageDescription: The cohort is excessively violent and cruel.
BITD.FlawsUnreliableDescription: The cohort isn't always available, due to other obligations, stupefaction from their vices, etc.
BITD.FlawsWildDescription: The cohort is drunken, debauched, and loud-mouthed.
BITD.HarmImpairedDescription: The cohort operates with reduced quality (-1d).
BITD.HarmBrokenDescription: The cohort can't do anything until they recover.
BITD.HarmDeadDescription: The cohort is destroyed. You may spend Personnel equal to your Tier +2 to restore it, plus two downtime activities to recruit new fireteam members, or hire a new expert.
BITD.RollTokenDescription: If you want to pull the numbers from a character, select their Token first.
BITD.RollDoDireCritical: You do it and a moment of clarity helped you clear 1 Stress.
BITD.RollDoDireVehicleCritical: You do it. Your vehicle lights up as it recovers 1 Quirk of your choosing.
BITD.RollDireSuccess: You push through your stress. You are allowed to take another Dire Action.
BITD.RollDireVehicleSuccess: Your vehicle holds on as you push it past its limits. You are allowed to take another vehicle Dire Action.
BITD.RollDireFailure: You succumb under the stress of your situation, and suffer severe harm. You must retreat from the scene in bad shape, this was your last action.
BITD.RollDireVehicleFailure: Your vehicle pushed past its limits and becomes unresponsive. It suffers severe damage and must retreat from the scene in bad shape, this was your vehicle's last action. Your vehicle's internals creak as it gained one tick of Breakdown, and you must degrade the last Quirk you've used in some way.
BITD.RollPartialSuccessPositionControlled: You hesitate. Withdraw and try a different approach, or else do it with a minor consequence: a minor complication occurs, you have reduced effect, you suffer lesser harm, you end up in a risky position.
BITD.RollPartialSuccessPositionRisky: You do it, but there's a consequence: you suffer harm, a complication occurs, you have reduced effect, you end up in a desperate position.
BITD.RollPartialSuccessPosition: You do it, but there's a consequence: you suffer severe harm, a serious complication occurs, you have reduced effect.
BITD.RollFailurePositionControlled: You falter. Press on by seizing a risky opportunity, or withdraw and try a different approach.
BITD.RollFailurePositionRisky: Things go badly. You suffer harm, a complication occurs, you end up in a desperate position, you lose this opportunity.
BITD.RollFailurePositionDesperate: It's the worst outcome. You suffer severe harm, a serious complication occurs, you lose this opportunity for action.
BITD.RollDoWithIncreasedEffectCohort: The cohort does it with increased effect.
BITD.RollResistance:
You reduce or avoid the effects of the consequence (GM chooses).
You have suffered {stress} Stress.
BITD.RollResistanceCritical:
You reduce or avoid the effects of the consequence (GM chooses).
Clear 1 Stress.
BITD.RollThreatPeril: Suffer a reduced consequence or push yourself to avoid the threat.
BITD.RollThreatThreat: You suffer the consequence. If you push yourself, reduce the consequence.
BITD.ResistanceQuirksSuccess:
You reduce or avoid the effects of the consequence (GM chooses).
Expend {quirks} Quirk(s).
BITD.ResistanceQuirksFailure:
You do not have enough Quirks to reduce or avoid the effects of the consequence.
You need {quirks} Quirk(s), but you only have {currentQuirks}.
BITD.ActionsInsightInfo: Actions involving awareness and observation. Resist consequences from deceit or empathy
BITD.ActionsHuntInfo: For tracking and long distance precision. (ex: Follow a squad through a ruined city.)
BITD.ActionsStudyInfo: For inspecting details and evidence. (ex: Read technical manuals to find a weak point; Determine a statement's truth by scrutinizing the speaker.)
BITD.ActionsSurveyInfo: For observing surroundings and possible outcomes. (ex: Spot trouble before it happens; Discover an attacker's vulnerabilities.)
BITD.ActionsEngineerInfo: For adjusting and creating devices and mechanisms. (ex: Develop a non-contagious strain of the flu; Fix a broken door.)
BITD.ActionsProwessInfo: Actions involving physicality and strength. Resist consequences from bodily harm or exertion.
BITD.ActionsFinesseInfo: For using manual dexterity. (ex: Secretly draw a pistol; Hand off a key to a prisoner.)
BITD.ActionsProwlInfo: For moving with grace and power. (ex: Climb onto a mech in the middle of battle; Sneak past a security camera; Stab an unsuspecting foe.)
BITD.ActionsStruggleInfo: For engaging in close quarters combat. (ex: Restrain a panicking civilian; Exchange gunfire across rooftops.)
BITD.ActionsWreckInfo: For applying brute force. (ex: Lift a car off a wounded companion; Smash through a locked door.)
BITD.ActionsResolveInfo: Actions involving socializing and networking. Resist consequences from mental fortitude or stubbornness.
BITD.ActionsInterfaceInfo: For using complex digital equipment. (ex: Hack into a technologically advanced vehicle; Understand how strange tech works.)
BITD.ActionsCommandInfo: For compelling quick obedience. (ex: Lead a fireteam in combat; Strike fear in others.)
BITD.ActionsConsortInfo: For socializing with others. (ex: Gain access to resources, intel, people, or places.)
BITD.ActionsSwayInfo: For influencing through guile, charm, or debate. (ex: Negotiate better ceasefire terms; Convince a guard that you belong in a restricted area.)
BITD.ActionsExpertiseInfo: Actions testing the vehicle's physicality. Resist consequences from a machine being damaged or strained.
BITD.ActionsBattleInfo: For attacking at short range with your vehicle. (ex: Lash out with your scoop arm; Fire your gatling gun in a dogfight.)
BITD.ActionsDestroyInfo: For razing structures with unsubtle methods with your vehicle. (ex: Carpet bomb a military base; Smash a building with a wrecking ball.)
BITD.ActionsManeuverInfo: For moving swiftly and precisely with your vehicle. (ex: Fly through the valley between skyscrapers; Creep across an unsteady bridge.)
BITD.ActionsAcuityInfo: Actions using the vehicle's awareness. Resist consequences from a machine being hacked or disoriented.
BITD.ActionsBombardInfo: For blasting with long-range fire with your vehicle. (ex: Designate long range missile targets; Shoot a railgun through a skyscraper at an enemy.)
BITD.ActionsManipulateInfo: For deftly handling objects with your vehicle. (ex: Remove a primed warhead from a missile; Pick up a baby in its carriage.)
BITD.ActionsScanInfo: For quickly assessing a situation with your vehicle. (ex: Identify the model of approaching vehicles; Spot the heat signature of an active generator.)
BITD.ForcePosition: Force?
BITD.ForceEffect: Force?
BITD.EffectWarning: Managing Active Effects within an Owned Item is not currently supported by FoundryVTT and will be added in a subsequent update.
BITD.Name: Name
BITD.SparkUsedText: Inspiration strikes you. Your determination to get this job done allows you to

SparkOptions.Adaptable1: Uninstall up to two points of your vehicle's Load
SparkOptions.Adaptable2: Push yourself in a vehicular maneuver
SparkOptions.RainyDay1: Gain a Supply Point to be used immediately
SparkOptions.RainyDay2: Push yourself when trying to bypass or enforce protocol
SparkOptions.EverybodyHurts1: Resist a consequence from ambushes (physical or not)
SparkOptions.Subterfuge1: Resist a consequence from suspicion or persuasion
SparkOptions.Warded2: Push yourself when you deal with AR forces
SparkOptions.Shadow1: Resist a consequence from detection or security measures
SparkOptions.Shadow2: Push yourself for a feat of athletics or stealth
SparkOptions.Mastermind2: Push yourself when you gather information
SparkOptions.Focused1: Resist a consequence of surprise or mental harm (fear, confusion, losing track of someone)
SparkOptions.Focused2: Push yourself for ranged combat or exploration
SparkOptions.Battleborn1: Reduce Harm from an attack in combat
SparkOptions.Fortitude1: Reduce a consequence of fatigue, weakness or chemical effects
SparkOptions.Fortitude2: Push yourself when working with technical skill or handling chemicals

BITD.SupplyRollFull: Supply
BITD.CollectDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a known Region here to set it as the Region to collect from.
BITD.SalvageDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a known Vehicle here to set it as the Vehicle to salvage.
BITD.SchmoozeDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a known Faction here to set it as the Faction to improve relationship with.
BITD.SupplyDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a known Faction here to set it as the Faction to gain supplies from.
BITD.TargetFactionDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a Faction here to set it as the Faction who was the target for that mission.
BITD.EmployerFactionDragDropInfo: Drag & drop a Faction here to set it as the Faction who issued that mission to the squad.
BITD.CarryThatWeightPushYourself: Carry That Weight: Push Yourself for +1d
BITD.CarryThatWeightAssist: Carry That Weight: Assist for Bonuses
BITD.LeaderTextStress: The leader of this group action, {leader}, suffered {stress} Stress due to failed actions.
BITD.LeaderTextQuirk: The leader of this group action, {leader}, expends {quirks} Quirk(s) due to failed actions.
BITD.RollFortuneCritical: The result is exceptional. You have great or extreme effect.
BITD.RollFortuneSuccess: The result is good. You have standard or full effect.
BITD.RollFortunePartialSuccess: The result is mixed. You have limited or partial effect.
BITD.RollFortuneFailure: The result is bad. You have poor or little effect.
BITD.RollGatherInformationSuccess: Great: You get exceptional details. The information is complete and follow-up questions may expand into related areas or reveal more than you hoped for.
BITD.RollGatherInformationPartialSuccess: Standard: You get good details. Clarifying and follow-up questions are possible.
BITD.RollGatherInformationFailure: Limited: You get incomplete or partial information. More information gathering will be needed to get all the answers.
BITD.RollEngagementCritical: Exceptional result. You've already overcome the first obstacle and you're in a controlled position for what's next.
BITD.RollEngagementSuccess: Good result. You're in a controlled position when the action starts.
BITD.RollEngagementPartialSuccess: Mixed result. You're in a risky position when the action starts.
BITD.RollEngagementFailure: Bad result. You're in a desperate position when the action starts.
BITD.RollSupplyResult: You have gained {supplyPoints} Supply Point(s), which may be turned into Materiel or Personnel Points at your leisure.

1.Title: Flipped OR roll on Table B
1.Description: A rival squad arranges for one of your allies, sponsors, clients, or a group of your customers to switch allegiances due to the attention on you. They're loyal to another Faction now. You may instead roll on Table B if you prefer.

2.Title: Missing Supplies
2.Description: Your supplier didn't give you the supplies you requested, citing rerouted logistics. Lose 3 Supply Points of any type, or try to browbeat logistics into giving you what you deserve with a Fortune Roll (1-3: Lose 2 Trust with the Faction that last gave the squad a Supply Roll, 4/5: Lose 1 Trust instead, 6: no Trust lost) to see if they squeal.

3.Title: Toxic Gear
3.Description: Scuttlebutt says the gear you were issued is unhealthy to be around. You may spend 1 Personnel for +1d, but either way make a Fortune Roll using the squad's Tier (1-3: Everyone takes a Level 1 Harm which can be resisted as normal, 4/5: Everyone spends 1 Materiel to trade it away or suffer 2 Stress, 6: It's not toxic).

4.Title: Desperate Civilians
4.Description: A group of civilians mob the squad, demanding supplies. Give them 4 Supply Points or make a Fortune Roll (1-3: Everyone takes two Level 1 Harm, 4/5: Lose 2 Supply Points) to see how bad things get.

5.Title: SCRAMBLE!
5.Description:
A squad you have negative Status with makes a move against you (or a friend, ally, or other associate). The attack comes suddenly and with little warning, prepare to fight or scatter and return after the attackers have done their damage.

If you prepare to fight, skip the Downtime Phase and immediately start a mission where the squad's FOB is under attack. Take into consideration the Hidden, Secure, and Quarters FOB Upgrades for the engagement roll. After the mission, follow the end of mission process as normal, including entanglements.

If you scatter, lose 2 Squad Upgrades as the attackers destroy large sections of your FOB.

6.Title: Show of Force
6.Description: A Faction with whom you have a negative Relationship wants to teach you a lesson. Give them 1 Squad Upgrade or gain Vendetta (drop to -3 Status) with two of their squads that you currently do not have Vendetta with. If you have no Upgrades, lose 1 Hold instead.

1.Title: AI Attention OR roll on Table C
1.Description:
An AI takes notice of your squad's actions and offers to cut a sinister deal with the squad. Choose one of the following:
  • Accept the bargain,
  • Hide until it loses interest (lose 3 Rep),
  • Deal with it another way.

You may instead roll on Table C if you prefer.

2.Title: Fire Team Trouble
2.Description:
One of your fire teams (or other Cohorts) causes trouble due to their Flaw(s). Choose one of the following:
  • You can lose face (lose Rep equal to your Tier +1),
  • Make an example of a fire team,
  • Face reprisals from the wronged party.

3.Title: Lowest Bidder
3.Description: Turns out your squad's gear was manufactured by the lowest bidder. All vehicles immediately suffer the Level 2 Damage: Busted and Level 1 Damage: Uncalibrated (each of which can be resisted separately), or each Pilot can spend 1 Materiel to get the good stuff through back channels.

4.Title: Challengers
4.Description: A squad with Status zero throws their weight around. They threaten you, a friend, an ally, or other associate. Lose 1 Rep or 1 Personnel per Tier of the rival, or stand up to them and lose 1 Status with them.

5.Title: Debriefing
5.Description: One of your superiors questions your squad. They grill one of your Pilots or a Cohort. Make a Fortune Roll using the Squad's Tier to see how well they resist the pressure (1-3: Level 2 Harm which can be resisted by Pilots as normal, 4/5: Lose 2 Trust), or bribe the grunts who grab the person by spending 1 Personnel.

6.Title: Requisition
6.Description:
A Cohort, Squad Upgrade, or acquired asset is requisitioned by another squad. Choose one of the following:
  • Give up the item (Gain 2 Trust with that squad's Patron Faction). You'll have to get it back later through the normal methods,
  • Bribe the caller by spending Personnel equal to your Tier +3,
  • Hide the object or fight back, and lose 1 Status with that squad AND 1 Relationship with their Faction.

If you don't have any Cohorts, Upgrades, or assets, you must select an option other than giving up the item (as the caller believes you definitely have it).

1.Title: New Recruit
1.Description: Someone the squad wants safe at home is assigned to them as a new recruit. Describe who this person is and the details of their bond with the squad. Gain a Specialist with Loyal and Eager. Their Quality is equal to the Squad's Tier -1 (minimum 0).

2.Title: Civil Unrest
2.Description:
A group of civilians riot due to perceived military corruption. Choose one of the following:
  • Spend Supply Points equal to the number of your FOB Squad Upgrades.
  • Make a Fortune Roll using your Tier to put it down (1-3: All Pilots take Level 1 Harm: Roughed Up, 4/5: Cohorts and Personnel can't be used in the next downtime and mission).
  • Let it run its course (regions your patron controls lose 1 Wealth to a minimum of 0).

3.Title: Cooperation
3.Description:
A squad with +3 Status asks for a favor. Choose one of the following:
  • Agree to do it.
  • Lose 1 Rep per Tier of the friendly squad.
  • Lose 1 Status with them.

This favor must be completed by the end of the next mission, and may take the form of a Long-Term Project, mission objective, or secondary objective. If you don't have a Squad with +3 Status, you avoid entanglements right now.

4.Title: Letter From Home
4.Description: One of the squad's Pilots receives a personal message with bad news. They must use a Downtime Activity to Cut Loose or suffer 3 Stress.

5.Title: Embedded Favourite
5.Description: The squad is burdened with an NPC that a superior favors and believes needs first hand field experience. The NPC must accompany you on your next mission and come back in one piece. If you refuse to take them along, lose 2 Trust with the Faction who favors them. If the NPC dies, lose 3 Trust. If they return injured, lose 1 Trust. The NPC is a temporary Specialist Cohort with Quality equal to the squad's Tier -1.

6.Title: Brass-holes
6.Description: Your superiors issue mission objectives that don't make sense to the squad. The squad takes -1d on their next Engagement Roll. Additionally, roll 1d to determine the tactic for the next mission (1: assault, 2: deception, 3: social, 4: transport, 5: science, 6: stealth). If the squad decides not to use that tactic, lose 1 Relationship with the Faction that assigned the mission (unless the mission is Independent).

BITD.RollEntanglementSlippery: Thanks to your squad being slippery, you must roll a second entanglement and pick the one you prefer among the two.
BITD.RollAcquireAssetResultSuccess: You gain an asset of Tier {tier_quality} quality.
BITD.RollAcquireAssetResultFailure: It was unfortunately not enough. You could not gain an asset of Tier {success_tier} quality or above. If you want to acquire it anyway, since your current asset is of Tier {tier_quality} quality, you need to spend {materiel} Materiel in total.
BITD.RollAcquireAssetExtra: To raise the result of this roll, you may spend 1 Materiel per Tier level, or raise it beyond critical success by spending 2 Materiel per additional level added.
BITD.RollCollect: You have collected a total of {supply_points} Supply Point(s) from {region}, which may be turned into Materiel or Personnel Points at your leisure.
BITD.RollCollectEntanglement: Since you have rolled at least one 1, you must immediately roll an Entanglement from table {entanglement}.
BITD.RollCollectBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Personnel, you may take an extra Collect activity if you so wish.
BITD.RollCutLoose:
You cut loose with {pilot}.
You cleared {clear_stress} Stress.
BITD.RollCutLooseSavedByCarouse: Your carousing lowered your Stress recovery, but prevented you from overindulging.
BITD.RollCutLooseCarouseStress: Your carousing lowered your Stress recovery.
BITD.RollCutLooseSavedByConviction: You gathered a significant data set, and didn't overindulge thanks to your focus on experimentation.
BITD.RollCutLooseConviction: Your focus on experimentation helped you gather a significant data set.
BITD.RollCutLooseFunctioningVice: Your functioning vice allows you to manually adjust the amount of stress recovered by up to +2 or -2.
BITD.RollCutLooseSavedByFunctioningVice: Your functioning vice prevented you from overindulging by {num}, since it allows you to manually adjust the amount of stress recovered by up to +2 or -2.
BITD.RollCutLooseDoubleFailure: You cut loose with {pilot} and both of you overindulge. You cleared {clear_stress} Stress and choose one of the following consequences which apply to both Pilots:
BITD.RollCutLooseFailure: You cut loose with {pilot} and overindulge. You cleared {clear_stress} Stress and choose one of the following consequences:
BITD.RollCutLooseFailureAttactTrouble: Attract Trouble. Roll an additional entanglement using the table and dice from the end of the previous mission.
BITD.RollCutLooseFailureBrag: Brag about your exploits. Lose 2 Trust with your Patron Faction.
BITD.RollCutLooseFailureLost: AWOL. Your Pilot vanishes for a few weeks. Play a different Pilot until this one returns from their 'vacation'. When your Pilot returns, they've also healed all their Harm.
BITD.RollCutLooseFailureTapped: Impropriety. Make an ass out of yourself in front of the Pilot that joined you-reset your Connection Clock with them to 0. If your Connection is already 0, take the Level 1 Harm: Alone.
BITD.RollCutLooseExtraResult: You may spend 1 Materiel to increase the result by 1.
BITD.RollCutLooseConnectionCarousePilot: Your bond with {pilot} grew a lot thanks to your carousing.
BITD.RollCutLooseConnectionCarouseOther: {pilot}'s bond with you grew thanks to your carousing.
BITD.RollCutLooseConnectionMaxxed: Your Connection Clock is full. Ask {pilot} for a truth about one belief you have about them. Your Connection Clock with them has been reset to 1 Tick as you see them in a new light. You both take 1 XP, which can go into either the Pilot's attribute or playbook XP track.
BITD.RollCutLooseConnectionCarouseOtherMaxxed: {pilot}'s Connection Clock is full. {pilot} asks for a truth about one belief they have about you. Their Connection Clock with you has been reset to 1 Tick as they see you in a new light. You both take 1 XP, which can go into either the Pilot's attribute or playbook XP track.
BITD.RollCutLooseBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Personnel, you may take an extra Cut Loose activity if you so wish.
BITD.RollCutLooseOtherPilot: Roll Stress Loss for the other Pilot
BITD.EnhanceText: You spend some time enhancing your vehicle. Your vehicle's Enhance tracker was increased by {num}.
BITD.EnhanceTextMaxxed: Your Enhance track is full. You may add a new Quirk to your vehicle OR add one Point to a Vehicle Action.
BITD.EnhanceBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Materiel, you may take an extra Enhance activity if you so wish.
BITD.RollFix: {fixActorName} spent some time fixing your vehicle.
BITD.RollFixStress: You suffered 2 Stress since your squad doesn't own a Workshop Squad Upgrade.
BITD.RollFixLevelOneDamage: All Level 1 Damage was cleared from your vehicle.
BITD.RollFixResult: Your Vehicle's Repair Clock was filled by {tick} tick(s).
BITD.RollFixResultFull: Since your Vehicle's Repair Clock is full, all Damage has been reduced by one level.
BITD.RollFixResultFullTwo: Since your Vehicle's Repair Clock filled twice, all Damage has been reduced by two levels.
BITD.RollFixExtraResult: You may spend 1 Materiel to increase the roll's result by 1.
BITD.RollFixBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Materiel, you may take an extra Fix activity if you so wish.
BITD.RollLongTermProject: You spent some time working on the Long-Term Project {ltp}. Its Progress Clock was filled by {tick} tick(s).
BITD.RollLongTermProjectIronsInTheFire: You spent some time working on Long-Term Projects {ltps}. {tick} tick(s) were distributed as evenly as possible across their Progress Clocks.
BITD.RollLongTermProjectIronsInTheFireRemaind: {tick} tick(s) could not be automatically distributed. Pick which Long-Term Project(s) they should be assigned to, with a max of one tick per project.
BITD.RollLongTermProjectIronsInTheFireOverTic: {tick} tick(s) could not be distributed because all projects were completed.
BITD.RollLongTermProjectExtraResult: You may spend 1 Personnel to increase the roll's result by 1.
BITD.RollLongTermProjectBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Personnel, you may take an extra Long-Term Project activity if you so wish.
BITD.RollManufactureResultSuccess: You manufacture an item of Tier {tier_quality} quality.
BITD.RollManufactureResultFailure: It was unfortunately not enough. You could not manufacture an item of Tier {success_tier} quality or above. If you want to manufacture it anyway, since your current item is of Tier {tier_quality} quality, you need to spend {materiel} Materiel in total.
BITD.RollManufactureExtra: To raise the result of this roll, you may spend 1 Materiel per Tier level, even beyond critical success.
BITD.RollRecover: {recoverActorName} spent some time treating your wounds.
BITD.RollRecoverNaturalRecoveryStress: You suffered 1 Stress since you are letting your wounds recover naturally.
BITD.RollRecoverSelfHealStress: You suffered 2 Stress since you are healing yourself as a doctor.
BITD.RollRecoverResult: Your Healing Clock was filled by {tick} tick(s).
BITD.RollRecoverResultFull: Since your Healing Clock is full, all Harm has been reduced by one level.
BITD.RollRecoverResultFullTwo: Since your Healing Clock filled twice, all Harm has been reduced by two levels.
BITD.RollRecoverExtraResult: You may spend 1 Personnel to increase the roll's result by 1.
BITD.RollRecoverBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Personnel, you may take an extra Recover activity if you so wish.
BITD.RollSalvage: You salvaged the vehicle {vehicle}. You destroyed the vehicle, collecting {supplyPoints} Supply Point(s) in the process, which may be turned into Materiel or Personnel Points at your leisure.
BITD.RollSalvageScroungers: You scrounged for resources. You collected {supplyPoints} Supply Point(s), which may be turned into Materiel or Personnel Points at your leisure.
BITD.RollSalvageBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Materiel, you may take an extra Salvage activity if you so wish.
BITD.RollSchmooze: You improved your squad {squad}'s relationship with the faction {faction} by {trust} Trust.
BITD.RollSchmoozeBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Personnel, you may take an extra Schmooze activity if you so wish.
BITD.TrainText: You spend some time training {trainTypeDesc}. Your {trainTypeText} XP was increased by {num}.
BITD.TrainLevelUpPlaybook: Your XP track has been filled. Take a new Playbook special ability.
BITD.TrainLevelUpAttribute: Your XP track has been filled. Add an Action Point under the {trainType} attribute.
BITD.TrainBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Personnel, you may take an extra Train activity if you so wish.
BITD.RollUpkeep: You spent some time recovering your vehicle's quirks. You used up {materiel} Materiel and recover {quirks} of your vehicle's quirks of your choosing.
BITD.RollUpkeepAll: You spent some time recovering your vehicle's quirks. You used up {materiel} Materiel and recovered ALL of your vehicle's quirks.
BITD.RollUpkeepExtraResult: You may spend a cumulative 1 Materiel per extra Quirk you wish to recover.
BITD.RollUpkeepBonusNotice: For the cost of 1 Materiel, you may take an extra Upkeep activity if you so wish.
BITD.RollMoveBase: You spent some time relocating your mobile FOB to a new location.
BITD.CollectionAgencyContents: Your collection agency has collected {value} Materiel for you.
BITD.SideBusinessContents: Your side business has collected {value} Personnel for you.
BITD.StressLossSuccess:
You cut loose with {pilot}.
You cleared {clear_stress} Stress.
BITD.StressLossFailure: You cut loose with {pilot} and both of you overindulge. You cleared {clear_stress} Stress and choose one of the following consequences which apply to both Pilots:
BITD.StayLateInfo:
You may provide a squadmate an extra Downtime Activity at the cost of taking the Level 1 Harm: Tired.
Choose the squadmate who will benefit from an extra Downtime Activity below.
BITD.StayLateDescription: {target} can take an extra Downtime Activity. {actor} takes the Level 1 Harm: Tired, which may be resisted as normal.
BITD.CarryThatWeightInfo:
When you have a personal moment with someone, you can take any amount of Stress
(up to your max Stress) to heal that person the same amount of Stress.
Choose the squadmate you will transfer Stress from.
BITD.CollateralDieEffect: You took a collateral die. Its consequences are unavoidable, nor can they be reduced or negated with a resistance roll or armor.
BITD.PushYourselfStressEffect: You pushed yourself. You suffered 2 Stress.
BITD.PushYourselfQuirkEffect: You pushed yourself. You must expend 1 Quirk.
BITD.AssistEffect:
{pilot} assisted you. They suffered {num} Stress. You have chosen the following {benefits} benefit(s):
    {effects}
BITD.AssistTacticalGeniusEffect:
{pilot} assisted you. They suffered no Stress due to their tactical genius. You have chosen the following {benefits} benefit(s):
    {effects}
BITD.DowntimeAssistOtherEffect: An ally or your direct superior assisted you. It is likely to provide access to resources otherwise unavailable.
BITD.SetupEffect: Another Pilot or a Cohort set you up for success. Your {effect} has improved.
BITD.TrustGain: Your squad gained {num} Trust with the faction {faction}.
BITD.TrustLoss: Your squad lost {num} Trust with the faction {faction}.
BITD.StatusUp: Your Trust has grown enough to increase your relationship with the faction {faction} to {relationship_level_string} ({relationship_level}).
BITD.StatusDown: Your Trust has lowered enough to decrease your relationship with the faction {faction} to {relationship_level_string} ({relationship_level}).
BITD.ReputationHoldUp: Your Reputation tracker is full, your Hold has increased to Strong.
BITD.ReputationNotifyTierUp: Your Reputation tracker is full, you may spend {cost} Materiel and Personnel to increase your squad's Tier by 1.
BITD.DisabledByAE: Minimum Value set by an Active Effect
BITD.MiddleClickForForm: Middle Click to assign this action point to a vehicle form.
BITD.AssignedFirstForm: This action point is only active when the vehicle is in its first form.
BITD.AssignedSecondForm: This action point is only active when the vehicle is in its second form.
BITD.FactionRelationshipInfo: Drag & drop a Faction, Squad, Character or NPC to add it as a relationship.
BITD.SquadRelationshipInfo: Drag & drop a Faction or Squad to add it as a relationship.
BITD.CharacterConnectionInfo: Drag & drop another Character or NPC to add it as a connection.
BITD.AddRegionOwnerInfo: Drag & drop a Faction or Squad here to assign it as this Region's owner.
BITD.Hidden: Hidden
BITD.StartMissionDescription:
This popup allows you to automate some parts of the mission starting process.
Any ticked checkbox will let the system automate that part of the process to the best of its ability.
This also serves as a cheat sheet: only green options have automation linked to them.
BITD.StartMissionPrepareMission:
The group covers the Rules of Engagement, the mission's target, objective, employer,
loadout, and a tactic (assault, deception, scientific, social, stealth, transport).
BITD.StartMissionRemainingDowntime: The Pilots may Gather Information and perform any last Downtime Activities if some remain.
BITD.StartMissionALittleSomethingOnTheSide:
A Little Something On The Side: The Pilot(s) {members} each roll a {tier}d Fortune roll.
On a result of 4+, add 1 tick to a Drive Clock.
BITD.StartMissionRefitStation: Refit Station: One Pilot may uninstall a piece of gear for free.
BITD.StartMissionNoCutLooseScar:
If any Pilot has one or several Scars and did not take a Cut Loose Downtime Activity,
they take Stress equal to the number of Scars they have,
unless they got a new Scar in the last mission.
Deselect the Pilots who got a Scar in the last mission!
BITD.StartMissionSupplyPoints: Any Supply Points not converted into Materiel or Personnel Points are lost.
BITD.StartMissionEngagement: The Pilots make an Engagement roll to move into the mission.
BITD.StartMissionNoCutLooseScarEffect: Some Pilots with scars have not taken a Cut Loose activity:
BITD.StartMissionRecoverSpark: All Pilots have recovered their spark.
BITD.StartMissionStart: The Mission starts now. Good luck!
BITD.EndMissionDescription:
This popup allows you to automate some parts of the mission ending process.
Any ticked checkbox will let the system automate that part of the process to the best of its ability.
This also serves as a cheat sheet: only green options have automation linked to them.
For anything related to Trust changes, enabling a bullet point means its effect is applied.
Only enable the ones that apply to the mission!
BITD.EndMissionSupplyRoll:
If the mission was successful, take a Supply Roll from the employer Faction. (Must be rolled manually)
If the mission's employer was Independent, {independentSupplyRoll}
BITD.EndMissionSupplyRollIndependentNone: do not make a Supply Roll.
BITD.EndMissionSupplyRollIndependent: thanks to friends in high places, make a 0d Supply Roll.
BITD.EndMissionFactionBonus:
Add your employer Faction's supply bonus to your squad's resources.
Bonus detected: {factionBonus}
BITD.EndMissionAirfield:
Airfield: Gain {num} Supply Point(s) (the automation just says it in the End Mission message,
the points have to be turned into Materiel or Personnel Points manually)
BITD.EndMissionSupplyPoints:
Assign the Supply Points as Materiel or Personnel.
Any Supply Points beyond the squad's maximum storage capacity must be used
by the end of the next Downtime phase, or they're wasted.
BITD.EndMissionPilotDrive:
Each Pilot checks to see if they did something that furthers their drive.
If they did, they add 1 Tick to a Drive Clock.
BITD.EndMissionRecoverFixUpkeep:
Every Pilot can spend Materiel then roll dice to refresh Quirks equal to the highest result.
Every Pilot removes all Level 1 Harm, and every Vehicle removes all Level 1 Damage.
Cohorts may heal one Level of Harm if the environment is suitable for healing. (Manual)
BITD.EndMissionFactionGoal:
If the mission supported the Patron Faction's goal, add 1 Tick to the goal's Clock.
If the Clock is filled, all squads patronized by the faction gain a benefit,
then choose a new Patron Faction goal.
BITD.EndMissionSquadStatus:
If a squad benefited from the mission, consider giving +1 to that squad's Status.
If a squad was harmed by the mission, consider giving -1 or -2 to that squad's Status depending on how bad the damage was.
For every squad Status point above or below zero, add a descriptor that expresses how that squad views the players' squad.
BITD.EndMissionScorchedEarthNote: NOTE: The Squad has Scorched Earth, meaning they shouldn't lose Trust when they cause property damage.
BITD.EndMissionSquadCasualties: At least one of their squads took casualties
BITD.EndMissionHighProfileTarget: Mission's target was high profile or well connected
BITD.EndMissionControlledTerritory: Attacked in a region they control
BITD.EndMissionVendetta: Vendetta with one of their squads at the beginning of the mission
BITD.EndMissionDireNeedsNote:
NOTE: The Squad has Dire Needs Earth, meaning they can choose not to lose Trust from breaking ROE
related to the treatment of civilians and enemy soldiers on a mission.
BITD.EndMissionHighSociety: High Society: +1 Trust (Only enable one of these options)
BITD.EndMissionNoTraces: No Traces: +1 Rep if no Trust loss from the Employer Faction
BITD.EndMissionBadImportantInfrastructure: Important infrastructure that was not the mission's target damaged
BITD.EndMissionEmployerSquadHarmed: A squad that was not the mission's target and belongs to the employer was harmed
BITD.EndMissionFromNoTraces: from your No Traces Squad Ability
BITD.EndMissionHealUp: All Pilots have had their Level 1 Harm removed, and all Vehicles have had their Level 1 Damage removed. Pilots may roll an Upkeep roll that doesn't count as a Downtime Activity if they so wish.
BITD.EndMissionAirfieldSupply: You have gained {num} Supply Point(s) from your Aircraft, remember to turn them into Materiel or Personnel Points before the end of Downtime.
BITD.EndMissionFactionGoalUp: The goal {goal} of your Patron Faction has progressed.
BITD.EndMissionFactionGoalDone: The goal has been completed, all squads patronized by the faction gain a benefit.
BITD.EndMissionVendettaEffect: Since the Squad is in Vendetta with the Squad(s) {vendettas}, the Pilots have less time to rest before the next mission.
BITD.EndMissionDowntimeStart: The Mission has ended, Pilots may now partake in Downtime Activities.
BITD.EndSessionDescription:
This sheet has no automation linked to it. It is merely just a cheat sheet for handling the end of a session.
Feel free to untick tasks you've already done as you go through them with your players!
BITD.EndSessionSquadXP: Check the squad's XP goals, and mark 1 XP for each item that occurred (or 2 XP if that item happened multiple times).
BITD.EndSessionRivalXP:
Check if each Pilot contributed at least 1 Tick to their Rival's Challenge Clock,
and whether they filled any Connection Clocks with their Rival or another Pilot.
Take 1 XP for each item that occurred.
BITD.EndSessionRivalDeath: Check if each Pilot permanently removed their Rival as a threat. If that happened, take 2 XP.
BITD.EndSessionPilotXP: Check the Pilots' XP goals, and mark 1 XP for each item that occurred (or 2 XP if that item happened multiple times).
BITD.EndSessionMissionComplete:
If a mission was completed this session, ask the Pilots the following questions to direct what the next mission will be:
  • Are you following up on a previous mission's outcome?
  • Is your aim to seize a Squad Upgrade, hurt the target, aid yourselves, or aid another squad?
  • Is there a specific Region you want to target?
  • Is there a specific resource you want to acquire?
  • Are you targeting a squad weaker, stronger, or about the same as you?
  • Are you targeting a specific squad or Faction?
BITD.EndSessionMentalCheck:
Check on each player's emotional state. Sometimes sessions can get very intense,
and players don't always realize how affected they are until they take a break.
If you're concerned about another player who says they're alright,
consider asking them privately in a couple of days once they've had a chance to assess their emotional state.
BITD.EndSessionLikeDislikeCheck:
Ask the players what they liked and disliked about the session, and what they want to see more or less of in future sessions.
If players can't think of anything, try asking in a couple of days after they've all had some time to sit with the session.
BITD.MultipleSelectUsage: Ctrl + Click to select several choices

AbilityUpgrade.AdvancedPrototypeTitle: Advanced Prototype: Pushing your Vehicle using an Experimental Gear?
AbilityUpgrade.Forgettable: Since you are forgettable, this interaction may be forgotten by the involved party until you interact with them again.
AbilityUpgrade.WorkHardPlayHard: The Pilot you Cut Loose with is allowed to make a free Stress Loss roll of their own, but if one of the Pilots overindulge, they both do.
AbilityUpgrade.Broadcast:
Since you pushed yourself, you can do one of the following in addition to the normal benefits:
  • Use your mind to instill a powerful, undirected emotion in others,
  • Paralyze a person with your mind or voice.
AbilityUpgrade.TelepathyTitle: Telepathy: Use the Leader's Action Rating instead of yours?
AbilityUpgrade.Telepathy:
Thanks to {owner}'s Telepathy, your skill for this action equals that of {leader}, the group action's leader.
{owner} also suffers 1 Stress.
AbilityUpgrade.WarlordTitle: Warlord: Involves a Faction your Squad has a Vendetta against?
AbilityUpgrade.WeavingTheWebGatherInfoTitle: Weaving The Web: Gathering Info on a Target for a Mission?
AbilityUpgrade.WeavingTheWebEngagementTitle: Weaving The Web: Roll for a Mission 'Weaving The Web' was used for previously?
AbilityUpgrade.TheDevilsFootsteps:
Since you pushed yourself, you can do one of the following in addition to the normal benefits:
  • Perform a feat of athletics that verges on the superhuman,
  • Maneuver to confuse your enemies so they mistakenly attack each other.
AbilityUpgrade.Sharpshooter:
Since you pushed yourself, you can do one of the following in addition to the normal benefits:
  • Make a ranged attack at extreme distance beyond what's normal for the weapon,
  • Unleash a barrage of rapid fire to suppress the enemy,
  • Ricochet a shot to hit a target indirectly.
AbilityUpgrade.NotToBeTrifledWith:
Since you pushed yourself, you can do one of the following in addition to the normal benefits:
  • Perform a feat of physical force that verges on the superhuman,
  • Engage a small gang on equal footing in close combat.
AbilityUpgrade.MeatIsCheapSaveTheMetalTitle: Meat Is Cheap, Save The Metal: Resisting from Vehicle Damage?
AbilityUpgrade.RedCometTitle: Red Comet: Trying to impress or dismay someone?
AbilityUpgrade.CookTheBooksTitle: Cook The Books: Roll Another Supply Roll for 2 Stress and 1 Downtime Activity?
AbilityUpgrade.ConnectedAllyTitle: Connected: Declaring an Ally During a Mission?
AbilityUpgrade.BeneathNoticeTitle: Beneath Notice: Trying to Confuse or Deflect Suspiscion from a Disguise or Covert Misdirection?
AbilityUpgrade.TrustInMeTitle: Trust In Me: Against a Target with whom You have a Strong Relationship?
AbilityUpgrade.CrowdsourceTitle: Crowdsource: Copy a Crewmate's Action Rating?
AbilityUpgrade.Crowdsource:
You crowdsourced the skill of {teammate} for this action.
You suffer 2 Stress.
AbilityUpgrade.MatrixMindTitle: Matrix Mind: Gathering Info about AR or Apps?
AbilityUpgrade.Tesla:
Since you pushed yourself, you can do one of the following in addition to the normal benefits:
  • Unleash a stroke of lightning as a weapon from the power grid,
  • Summon an AR storm in your immediate vicinity (swirling ads, flashing neon, heavy smog, shouting crowds, etc.) that will cause AR confusion.
AbilityUpgrade.AmbushTitle: Ambush: Attacking from Hiding or Springing a Trap?
AbilityUpgrade.NeverTellMeTheOddsTitle: Never Tell Me The Odds: Outnumbered or Drastically Outmatched by a Foe?
AbilityUpgrade.LayOfTheLandTitle: Lay Of The Land: Taking Advantage of Scouted Terrain?
AbilityUpgrade.RangerGatherInfoTitle: Ranger: Gathering Information to Locate a Target?
AbilityUpgrade.RangerActionTitle: Ranger: Trying to Avoid Detection while Hiding in a Prepared Position or Using Camouflage?
AbilityUpgrade.BodyguardGatherInfoTitle: Bodyguard: Gathering Information to Anticipate Possible Threats?
AbilityUpgrade.BrutalTitle: Brutal: Trying to Command a Frightened Target?
AbilityUpgrade.RobotFighterTitle: Robot Fighter: Fighting Against a Machine?
AbilityUpgrade.SaboteurExplosivesTitle: Saboteur: Using Placed Explosives Instead of Wrecking Tools?
AbilityUpgrade.Saboteur: Since you are a Saboteur, the work is much quieter than it should be and the damage is hidden from casual inspection.

VehicleGearUpgrade.BoostedReactorsTitle: Boosted Reactors: Use for Greater Speed or Agility?
VehicleGearUpgrade.BoostedReactors: Since you used your Boosted Reactors, they will only be usable again at the start of the next mission.
VehicleGearUpgrade.ScaryWeaponToolTitle: Scary Weapon or Tool: Used for Intimidation?

ItemUpgrade.Intel: You used your intel. You spent 1 Personnel.

SquadAbilityUpgrade.NobleOfficerTitle: Noble Officer: Challenging Someone to a Duel or Fighting Against Your Duel Opponent?

SquadAbilityUpgrade.BloodBrothersTitle: Blood Brothers: Fighting Alongside your Fire Teams in Combat?

SquadAbilityUpgrade.ShockAndAweTitle: Shock and Awe: Executing an Assault Plan?
SquadAbilityUpgrade.JustPassingThroughTitle: Just Passing Through: Deceiving People by Passing Yourself as Ordinary Citizens in a High Trust Faction Region?
SquadAbilityUpgrade.OnTheMoveGatherInfoTitle: On The Move: Gathering Information about Next Mission's Route?

SquadAbilityUpgrade.AirSuperiorityTitle: Air Superiority: Active Aircraft in Area Only on Your Side?
SquadAbilityUpgrade.CustomWorkTitle: Custom Work: Engineering one of Your Squad's Vehicles?
SquadAbilityUpgrade.Formation: You followed the formation. You suffered 1 Stress.
SquadAbilityUpgrade.ReaversTitle: Reavers: Pushing Yourself while Employing Speed or Aggression?
SquadAbilityUpgrade.Reavers: Since you are a Reaver, you do not exhaust a Quirk for failing this action.
SquadAbilityUpgrade.OfThePeoplePositionTitle: Of The People: Acting Against Someone who Wants what you are Selling?
SquadAbilityUpgrade.OfThePeopleGatherInfoTitle: Of The People: Gathering Information about Where your Product is Wanted?

SquadAbilityUpgrade.SecondStoryTitle: Second Story: Executing a Clandestine Infiltration?

SquadUpgrade.RepairBayLongTermProjectTitle: Repair Bay: Long-Term Project Involves Vehicles or Vehicle Gear?

SquadUpgrade.LoyalBarTitle: Loyal Bar: Gathering Information about Current or Upcoming Mission?
SquadUpgrade.TransmitterArrayTitle: Transmitter Array: Suffering Stress from a Digital Power or App Usage?

OtherModifier.AcquireAssetDangerTitle: Acquire Asset: Acquiring a Chemical, Poison or Dangerous Gadget?

OtherModifier.AcquireAssetGearTitle: Acquire Asset: Acquiring Pilot/Vehicle Gear: Spend {num} Region Faction Trust for +{num}d?

OtherModifier.CollectBonusRoll: You spent 1 Personnel to get an extra Collect roll.
OtherModifier.CutLooseBonusRollTitle: Cut Loose: Spend 1 Personnel for an Extra Cut Loose Roll?
OtherModifier.CutLooseBonusRoll: You spent 1 Personnel to get an extra Cut Loose roll.
OtherModifier.CutLooseExtraDie: You spent 1 Personnel while cutting loose.
OtherModifier.EnhanceBonusRollTitle: Enhance: Spend 1 Materiel for an Extra Enhance Activity?
OtherModifier.EnhanceBonusRoll: You spent 1 Materiel to get an extra Enhance activity.
OtherModifier.FixBonusRollTitle: Fix: Spend 1 Materiel for an Extra Fix Activity?

OtherModifier.LongTermProjectBonusRoll: You spent 1 Personnel to get an extra Long-Term Project activity.
OtherModifier.LongTermProjectExtraDie: You spent 1 Personnel while you were working on a Long-Term Project.

OtherModifier.RecoverBonusRoll: You spent 1 Personnel to get an extra Recover activity.
OtherModifier.RecoverExtraDie: You spent 1 Personnel while you were recovering.
OtherModifier.SalvageBonusRollTitle: Salvage: Spend 1 Materiel for an Extra Salvage Activity?
OtherModifier.SalvageBonusRoll: You spent 1 Materiel to get an extra Salvage activity.
OtherModifier.SchmoozeBonusRollTitle: Schmooze: Spend 1 Personnel for an Extra Schmooze Activity?
OtherModifier.SchmoozeBonusRoll: You spent 1 Personnel to get an extra Schmooze activity.

Load.Name: Alternate Load
Load.Hint: Replaces the base Load Rules (Light/Normal/Heavy) with the Load Rules (Discreet/Conspicuous) from the Deep Cuts supplement.

Edge.Name: Edge
Edge.Hint: Adds Edge to character sheets from the Deep Cuts supplement.

PublicClocks.Name: Default Observable Clocks
PublicClocks.Hint: All newly made Clock actors will have permissions set to Observer for all players.

ClockStyles.Name: Clock Styles
ClockStyles.Label: Configure Clock Styles...
ClockStyles.Hint:
List of all detected clock styles that can be used.
System clock styles are stored in your Foundry folder's systems/beamsaber/themes folder.
World clock styles are stored in your Foundry folder's worlds/<YourWorld>/themes folder.
ClockStyles.Fine1: All of the clocks sprites in this
ClockStyles.Fine2: are valid and fine to use
ClockStyles.IsShifted: Is Shifted?
ClockStyles.WrongExtension: Wrong file extension for fill {fill} (found .{bad}, looking for .{good})

DefaultClockThemeColor.Name: Default Clock Style & Color
DefaultClockThemeColor.Hint: Clock style and color used by default in sheets.

DowntimeRules.Name: Downtime Rules
DowntimeRules.Hint:
Whether not having any more Downtime Activities available prevents rolls which use them or not.
Strict: Rolls are prevented.
Lax: They are not.

ActorDragAndDrop.Name: Actor Drag & Drop
ActorDragAndDrop.Hint:
Indicates who is allowed to drag & drop actors. Note that this behavior is used for some rolls and features.
Everyone: Every user is allowed to drag and drop actors.
Trusted Players & GMs: Normal players are forbidden.
All Gamemasters: All players are forbidden.
Gamemaster: Only the main Gamemaster is allowed.

IncrementClock.Name: Increment Clock
IncrementClock.Hint:
Adds 1 tick to all selected clocks if they're not full.
Clocks are maxed if Shift is also pressed.

DecrementClock.Name: Decrement Clock
DecrementClock.Hint:
Rermoves 1 tick from all selected clocks if they're not empty.
Clocks are emptied if Shift is also pressed.

info.SameObjectFromOwner: The dropped {obj} already belongs to this {owner}.
info.OwnedFactionRelationship: The dropped actor is already owned by this faction.
info.SameFactionRelationship: The dropped actor already has a relationship with this faction.
info.SameSquadRelationship: The dropped actor already has a relationship with this squad.

warn.NotItemOwnerVehicle: The gear owned by this vehicle is internally owned by its pilot. Please open this vehicle's gear from its pilot' sheet instead.
warn.NoAddFromSuppressedContainer: Items cannot be added to a disabled container. Enable it first by changing the vehicle's current form.
warn.NoSquadToAddConnection: The Character needs a Squad in order to display a list of connections to add!

warn.WorkInProgress: This feature has currently been disabled as it is a work in progress!

warn.WorkHardPlayHardNoActor: You currently have no character assigned! Select a Token first or get a character assigned by your GM.
warn.WorkHardPlayHardWrongActor: The 'Work Hard, Play Hard' Stress Loss roll awaits the Pilot {name}, but {currentName} is selected.
warn.UnknownRollType: The roll type "{type}" isn't recognized as a valid roll type.

warn.TryCreateNoActiveGM: Some data could not be created due to having no Gamemaster or Assistant Gamemaster currently connected. It will be created once one of them connects to the server.

warn.TryDeleteNoActiveGM: Some data could not be deleted due to having no Gamemaster or Assistant Gamemaster currently connected. It will be deleted once one of them connects to the server.
warn.NoClockImage: There is no existing clock image for the theme {theme}, color {color}, size {size} and fill {fill}.
warn.MustOwnCharacterAndVehicle: You must own both the Character and Vehicle in order to link or unlink them.
